author: @pattypoehls6346
text: I have a hard time typing without  typos on teeny tabldt, with tiny ldtters, lol. So, hi, I deleted  my comment  of how I love sunflower  seed oil, but use it in a blend of oils. this is a hobby for me, not a job. some soapers, particularly  the  professional  soapers have accumulated  such an  array of oils, scents, colourants, and otger soaping  additives from luxery ingredients like silk, butters and  food based ingredients, or clays, pummice, salts, etc. I think most hobby soapers start with very basic ingredients, knowing that its also  important  to  me,  you, and most people WHAT IS NOT IN OUR SOAP, OR WHAT HASNT BEEN TAKEN OUT TO MAKE 2 PRODUCTS OUT OF ONE and big profit driven companies  will often compromise  on quality  to get a fatter profit...not to mention, they put you on the merry go round of your skin dried out, so now you use their lotions, body washes, etc.   A  real bar of soap can clean from head to toe, it can clean your kitchen, and your clothes.  My mother in law once said to me, even a poor person can afford a bar of soap, and she said you can keep almost anything clean with soap. That's  an over simplification but I think it lead me to my interest in soaps. I have a theory soap started  even in ancient times....knowledge  comes and goes.... thanks for your interesting video!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @Shanjaq
text: it verges on Impossible to obtain isolated sodium hydroxide in the 'states, not even with drain cleaner.. any suggestions for an alternative?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
you can collect it from wood ash by steeping the ash in water and drying it out. Drill a lot of holes in the bottom of a small wooden barrel, make sure it's waterproof before you drill the holes!
					
					Stand the barrel on blocks leaving space beneath the barrel for a container. Use a waterproof wood or glass container. Lye can burn through some metals.
					
					Put a layer of gravel in the bottom of the barrel over the holes, then put a layer of straw over the gravel. Fill the rest of the barrel with hardwood ash (NOTE: hardwood -- NOT softwood), leaving a couple of inches at the top clear. Then pour rainwater into the barrel. After a long time the water in the barrel will start to drip into the container. Leave it until it stops, then replace the container with another in case of odd drips.
					
					Use an old iron pot, or a steel pan (One you will not be using for anything else!). Boil the liquid until it is so concentrated that a fresh egg (still in it's shell please!) will float on top. Then destroy the egg. Remember to take all precautions not the let the liquid touch your skin or clothing.

author: @MongrelShark
text:
Sodium Hydroxide. The ultimate exfoliator. If You can reduce the eye sting, you could be onto something there. Will it get rid of my dandruff?

When I was making large 10L batches of laundry soap (liquid), I used a 600w stick mixer to get to trace. Much easier on the elbow grease.
I also made my own essence by boiling quartered oranges and lemongrass. Bought essences didn't seem to work as well for grease cutting and where expensive Cost twice as much as the rest of the ingredients combined to actually add scent to 10L batch. Could you do a video on making your own essence?

I was under the impression you should check ph during curing, and when it stops reducing, your cured. Some soaps seem to take longer than others, also depends on block size I think. I never made hard soap.

I'll go find my old recipe book and see if it has the formula I used.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I like the essence making idea - a lot and the use of a stick mixer - good idea and thanks for sharing. Checking pH is just something to do at sometime and you are right when pH is less than 8 you are done. Pretty  much as long as you are careful with the lye nothing much can go wrong here

replies.author: @MongrelShark
replies.text:
So is that a no to high ph dandruff shampoo?

The essence did something really cool too. After boiling the oranges and lemongrass for a few hours in a 3L pot. I removed the large bits of orange and left the finely chopped lemongrass (bought in dry stick form) and orange pulp in. The liquid was clear but a lot thicker than water. similar viscosity to baby oil. I then added it hot to alreay traced cold mix formula soap, which was made with graded solid soap, oil, letric soda (washing soda) and borax. The white laundry liquid and the clear essence turned the whole thing orange when I mixed it in with a similar method to tracing. Pretty much had to re-trace as it would go lumpy if I didn't. Neded about half a cup for 7kg top loader of hevily soiled work clothes, but cost about $4 (2-3 UK pounds) to make 10 liters. All my vegan mates loved it.

author: @MYNICEEV
text: Oil be dammed. What an amazing tutorial. I too was wondering about engine oil recycled. Using a magnet to get out any metal from the engine. Petrol? Those sales could go with a bang. LOL.

replies.author: @MongrelShark
replies.text: I dont think it works with mineral oil. LOL. Although if you did make a soap with "Minerals" it would probably sell well. XD If you left the metal bits in you could compete with Solvol.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Mongrel Shark
You can do it with mineral oil but not by using a simple mix with lye method. This only works with glycerides - which is what natural fats and oils are. Mineral oils are long chain hydrocarbons and need to be  converted into suitable forms first. You can do this with mineral acids and refluxing and then mixing with lye a la US patent number 1577723 - but i thought that was a bit beyond a basics tutorial

replies.author: @MongrelShark
replies.text: Yep, so far beyond basic it made a swooshing sound a few feet above my head.
